title: Did the Quran Predict the SEVEN Layers of the Sky?

sound.ambient: deep space cosmic ambience, low airy hum, vast and clean, subtle shimmer, no music, no melody, no instruments

_note: Sequel to the Seven Earths hit. Story structure with tension + a specific discovery moment. Continuous flow (gap 0.1), micro-pauses via ellipses, fast scene cuts, ~140s. No em dashes. No recitation = copyright-free. Voice: Oliver Silk.

segments.text: Fourteen centuries ago, the Quran declared the sky above you is not one… but seven.
segments.caption: The sky is not one.

segments.text: Scientists believed the atmosphere was a single blanket of air, until balloons and rockets revealed the shocking truth.
segments.caption: They were wrong.

segments.text: What they found will change the way you look up… forever.
segments.caption: Look up.

segments.translation: [He] who created seven heavens in layers.
segments.text: The Quran states. He who created seven heavens, in layers. Surah Al-Mulk, verse three.
And the Prophet, peace be upon him, spoke of seven skies, one above the other. For over a thousand years… it seemed impossible.
segments.caption: Seven skies.

segments.text: How could the sky have seven layers, when all we see is open blue?
segments.caption: How?

segments.text: For most of history, there was no way to climb above the clouds. No way to measure what shields us from the cold of space.
segments.caption: A sealed mystery.

segments.text: Then, in 1902, a French scientist named Léon Teisserenc de Bort sent weather balloons higher than anyone had ever dared.
And he found something no one expected. Far above the air we breathe… lay a second, completely different layer.
segments.caption: A second layer.

segments.text: The sky was not one. It was many.
segments.caption: It was many.

segments.text: Today, science divides the sky into seven layers.
segments.caption: Seven layers.

segments.text: The troposphere. The stratosphere. The ozone layer. The mesosphere. The thermosphere. The ionosphere. And the exosphere.
segments.caption: Seven.

segments.text: Seven heavens… exactly as it was written, fourteen centuries ago.
segments.caption: Exactly as written.

segments.text: And each one guards the Earth from a different danger above.
segments.caption: Each one a shield.

segments.text: So here is the question that should stop you.
segments.caption: The question.

segments.text: How did a man in seventh century Arabia know the sky was built in seven layers… when no human alive could rise high enough to see even one?
segments.caption: How did he know?

segments.text: Perhaps some knowledge does not come from looking up… but from the One who raised what is above us.
segments.caption: From the One above.

segments.text: Follow Ketabi Studio, for more truths that science keeps catching up to… one sign at a time.
segments.caption: Follow Ketabi Studio.

segments.text: And our app, Ketabi, is a calm home for your faith. Get it free, at Ketabi Studio dot com.
segments.caption: Download Ketabi.

sources: Quran 67:3 (Surah Al-Mulk) - 'who created seven heavens in layers' (tibaqan)
Atmospheric science - the atmosphere is commonly divided into layers (troposphere, stratosphere, mesosphere, thermosphere, exosphere; with ozone layer and ionosphere)
